classObjective: Choose appropriate measures of center and spread based on whether a distribution is symmetric or skewed.
workedExamples: 9-5, Example 3 — Describe Center and Spread Using a Box Plot
9-5, Example 4 — Analyze Outliers and Their Effects
warmUp: For a symmetric distribution, which measure of center is most representative?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: mean/std dev for symmetric distributions; median/IQR for skewed distributions; outlier effect analysis
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice choosing appropriate statistics based on distribution shape
independentPractice: Practice problems analyzing distributions, identifying outliers, and selecting appropriate statistics
assessment: Exit ticket: a data set has a strong outlier — which measures of center and spread would you use and why?
capReflection: Why is mean/std dev inappropriate for heavily skewed distributions?
